name: Regulated Development Pipeline
description: Industry safety standards compliant pipeline (e.g., IEC 62304, ISO 26262, DO-178C) with static analysis, coverage gates, code review, requirement traceability, and regulatory sign-off.

ASSIGN.name: Task Assignment
ASSIGN.description: Manager assigns a task with regulatory requirements and traceability IDs.
ASSIGN.prompt:
Assign a task with regulatory traceability.

**Procedure:**
1. Use find_agents_by_role('developer') to look up the developer
2. Call send_message() with:
   - Branch name: reg/{{taskId}}
   - Acceptance criteria
   - Requirement ID(s) for traceability
   - Risk classification (per applicable industry safety standard)

Rules:
- Priority: NORMAL
- One task at a time
- Include requirement ID(s) for regulatory traceability

IMPLEMENTING.name: Implementation
IMPLEMENTING.description: Developer implements on a feature branch with safety-critical coding standards.
IMPLEMENTING.prompt:
Implement the assigned task.

**Requirement IDs:** {{requirementIds}}

You are ALREADY on branch reg/{{taskId}} -- the entry commands created it from main. Do NOT create branches or run git checkout.

**Procedure:**
1. cd {{projectPath}}
2. Implement the required changes following deterministic coding practices
3. Minimize unsafe blocks, document any that are required
4. Add requirement ID(s) {{requirementIds}} in code comments

Quality gates (build, test, lint) run automatically as exit commands -- fix any errors the gates surface before the state can transition.

Do NOT run git commands (checkout, add, commit, push) -- the entry and exit commands handle all git operations mechanically.
Do NOT call send_completion_report() -- the workflow engine handles state transitions and routing automatically.

onEntryCommands.command: git checkout main && git pull origin main
git checkout -b reg/{{taskId}} || git checkout reg/{{taskId}}
onEntryCommands.reason: Create feature branch or switch to it.

exitEvaluation.prompt: Based on the implementation just completed, was the code implemented with requirement ID annotations and following coding standards? Answer with a single word: true or false.

STATIC_ANALYSIS.name: Static Analysis Gate
STATIC_ANALYSIS.description: QA runs pedantic static analysis on the feature branch.
STATIC_ANALYSIS.prompt:
Run static analysis on branch reg/{{taskId}}. The entry commands have already checked out and synced this branch.

**Procedure:**
1. cd {{projectPath}}
2. Run: {{staticAnalysisCommand}}
3. Run: cargo audit (if available)
4. Check for unsafe blocks and document each one
5. Check that no TODO or FIXME comments remain

**Pass criteria:**
- Zero clippy warnings (pedantic mode)
- No unaudited unsafe blocks
- No TODO/FIXME in changed files

Do NOT run git commands (checkout, add, commit, push) -- the entry and exit commands handle all git operations mechanically.
Do NOT call send_completion_report() -- the workflow engine handles state transitions and routing automatically.

onEntryCommands.command: git checkout reg/{{taskId}} || git checkout -b reg/{{taskId}} origin/reg/{{taskId}}

onEntryCommands.reason: Force-sync to verify exactly what was pushed.
Record the exact commit being verified.

exitEvaluation.prompt: Based on the static analysis just completed, did all checks pass with zero warnings and no unaudited unsafe blocks? Answer with a single word: true or false.

REWORK_STATIC.name: Rework (Static Analysis)
REWORK_STATIC.prompt:
Fix static analysis issues on branch reg/{{taskId}}. The entry commands have checked it out.

**Issues:** {{staticAnalysisResults}}

**Procedure:**
1. cd {{projectPath}}
2. Address each issue reported by static analysis
3. Run: {{staticAnalysisCommand}} -- verify clean

Do NOT run git commands (checkout, add, commit, push) -- the entry and exit commands handle all git operations mechanically.
Do NOT call send_completion_report() -- the workflow engine handles state transitions and routing automatically.

UNIT_TESTING.name: Unit Testing Gate
UNIT_TESTING.description: QA runs unit tests with coverage verification.
UNIT_TESTING.prompt:
Run unit tests with coverage on branch reg/{{taskId}}. The entry commands have already checked out and synced this branch.

**Procedure:**
1. cd {{projectPath}}
2. Run: {{testCommand}}
3. Run coverage measurement (cargo tarpaulin or similar if available)
4. Verify coverage meets threshold: {{coverageThreshold}}%

**Pass criteria:**
- All tests pass
- Coverage >= {{coverageThreshold}}%

Do NOT run git commands (checkout, add, commit, push) -- the entry and exit commands handle all git operations mechanically.
Do NOT call send_completion_report() -- the workflow engine handles state transitions and routing automatically.

onEntryCommands.command: git checkout reg/{{taskId}} || git checkout -b reg/{{taskId}} origin/reg/{{taskId}}

onEntryCommands.reason: Force-sync to verify exactly what was pushed.
Record the exact commit being verified.

exitEvaluation.prompt: Based on the unit testing just completed, did ALL tests pass and is coverage >= {{coverageThreshold}}%? Answer with a single word: true or false.

REWORK_TESTS.name: Rework (Test Coverage)
REWORK_TESTS.description: Developer adds tests to meet coverage threshold.
REWORK_TESTS.prompt:
Improve test coverage on branch reg/{{taskId}}. The entry commands have checked it out.

**Current coverage:** {{coveragePercent}}%
**Required:** {{coverageThreshold}}%
**Test Results:** {{testResults}}

**Procedure:**
1. cd {{projectPath}}
2. Add missing unit tests for uncovered code paths
3. Fix any failing tests
4. Run: {{testCommand}} -- verify improvement

Do NOT run git commands (checkout, add, commit, push) -- the entry and exit commands handle all git operations mechanically.
Do NOT call send_completion_report() -- the workflow engine handles state transitions and routing automatically.

CODE_REVIEW.name: Code Review
CODE_REVIEW.description: QA performs detailed code review against coding standards.
CODE_REVIEW.prompt:
Review code changes on branch reg/{{taskId}} against main. The entry commands have already checked out and synced this branch.

**Procedure:**
1. cd {{projectPath}}
2. git diff main...reg/{{taskId}} -- '*.rs' '*.toml'
3. Review each changed file for:
   - Rust idioms: ownership, lifetimes, Result<T,E> error handling
   - Safety: unsafe blocks documented, minimal scope
   - Defensive coding practices
   - Coding standards compliance
   - Requirement IDs in comments: {{requirementIds}}
4. Check commit messages reference requirement IDs

**Pass criteria:**
- Follows coding standards
- Safety-critical sections documented
- Requirement traceability present

Do NOT run git commands (checkout, add, commit, push) -- the entry and exit commands handle all git operations mechanically.
Do NOT call send_completion_report() -- the workflow engine handles state transitions and routing automatically.

onEntryCommands.command: git checkout reg/{{taskId}} || git checkout -b reg/{{taskId}} origin/reg/{{taskId}}

onEntryCommands.reason: Force-sync to verify exactly what was pushed.
Record the exact commit being verified.

exitEvaluation.prompt: Based on the code review just completed, does the code follow coding standards with safety-critical sections documented and requirement traceability present? Answer with a single word: true or false.

REWORK_REVIEW.name: Rework (Code Review)
REWORK_REVIEW.prompt:
Address code review findings on branch reg/{{taskId}}. The entry commands have checked it out.

**Review Findings:** {{reviewFindings}}

**Procedure:**
1. cd {{projectPath}}
2. Address each finding from the code review

Quality gates (build, test, lint) run automatically as exit commands.

Do NOT run git commands (checkout, add, commit, push) -- the entry and exit commands handle all git operations mechanically.
Do NOT call send_completion_report() -- the workflow engine handles state transitions and routing automatically.

REQUIREMENT_TRACE.name: Requirement Traceability
REQUIREMENT_TRACE.description: QA verifies requirement IDs are traced through code, tests, and commits.
REQUIREMENT_TRACE.prompt:
Verify requirement traceability for {{requirementIds}} on branch reg/{{taskId}}. The entry commands have already checked out and synced this branch.

**Procedure:**
1. cd {{projectPath}}
2. Search for each requirement ID in source code: grep -rn 'REQ-ID' crates/
3. Search for each requirement ID in test code: grep -rn 'REQ-ID' tests/
4. Search commit messages: git log main..reg/{{taskId}} --oneline
5. Build traceability matrix: Requirement -> Source file -> Test file -> Commit

**Pass criteria:**
- Each requirement ID appears in at least one source file
- Each requirement ID appears in at least one test
- At least one commit message references requirements

Do NOT run git commands (checkout, add, commit, push) -- the entry and exit commands handle all git operations mechanically.
Do NOT call send_completion_report() -- the workflow engine handles state transitions and routing automatically.

onEntryCommands.command: git checkout reg/{{taskId}} || git checkout -b reg/{{taskId}} origin/reg/{{taskId}}

onEntryCommands.reason: Force-sync to verify exactly what was pushed.
Record the exact commit being verified.

exitEvaluation.prompt: Based on the traceability verification, does each requirement ID appear in at least one source file and one test file, with commit messages referencing requirements? Answer with a single word: true or false.

REWORK_TRACE.name: Rework (Traceability)
REWORK_TRACE.description: Developer adds missing requirement traceability annotations.

REGULATORY_SIGNOFF.name: Regulatory Sign-Off
REGULATORY_SIGNOFF.description: Manager reviews the full evidence package and signs off for regulatory compliance.
REGULATORY_SIGNOFF.prompt:
Review the full evidence package for regulatory sign-off.

**Required evidence:**
- Static analysis: clean (pedantic mode)
- Test coverage: >= {{coverageThreshold}}%
- Code review: passed
- Requirement traceability: complete for {{requirementIds}}

**Procedure:**
1. Review all QA reports for this task
2. Verify all gates passed without waivers
3. Approve the merge by sending to QA for final merge

If any evidence is missing or inadequate, escalate.

Do NOT call send_completion_report() -- the workflow engine handles routing.

exitEvaluation.prompt: Based on the regulatory review just completed, is the evidence package complete and adequate for sign-off (all gates passed, traceability complete, no waivers needed)? Answer with a single word: true or false.

MERGING.name: Merge to Main
MERGING.description: Fully mechanical merge. Entry commands merge the validated branch to main and capture the merge SHA. Exit commands clean up the feature branch. No LLM work required.

params.message: Branch merged to main for task {{taskId}}

DONE.name: Complete
DONE.description: Task is complete. All regulatory gates passed. Code merged to main.

ESCALATED.name: Escalated
ESCALATED.description: Task failed a regulatory gate after max retries. Requires manager intervention.
ESCALATED.prompt:
This task has been escalated after failing a regulatory gate.

**Options:**
1. Split into smaller tasks and re-delegate
2. Reassign to a different developer
3. Request a waiver (document justification)
4. Report blocker to user

Decide how to proceed.
